task: I’ve got a form and a deadline sitting in front of me, and I want to make sure I handle it the right way before I do anything with it. Can you help me think through what to check first and how to keep track of the parts that still need attention?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — a good way to start is to treat it like a quick triage.

### First, check these basics
1. **What kind of form is it?**  
   Is it for school, work, taxes, legal, medical, benefits, etc.? The type affects how careful you need to be.

2. **What is the deadline exactly?**  
   Note:
   - date
   - time
   - time zone, if relevant
   - whether it’s a submission deadline or a “must be completed by” deadline

3. **What happens if it’s late or incomplete?**  
   Look for:
   - penalties
   - missed eligibility
   - rejection
   - need for additional documents

4. **What are the required fields?**  
   Scan for:
   - mandatory vs optional sections
   - signatures/initials
   - attachments
   - supporting documents
   - formatting rules

5. **Who is it for and where does it go?**  
   Make sure you know:
   - the correct recipient
   - submission method
   - whether you need a copy for yourself

### How to track what still needs attention
A simple checklist works well. You can use something like:

- [ ] Read instructions fully
- [ ] Confirm deadline
- [ ] Gather required documents
- [ ] Fill out all required fields
- [ ] Review for errors
- [ ] Sign/date form
- [ ] Attach supporting materials
- [ ] Make a copy/save a copy
- [ ] Submit
- [ ] Confirm receipt

### A practical way to stay organized
If the form has multiple sections, make a small table:

| Section | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Personal info | Done / Needs review | Check spelling |
| Documents | Missing | Need ID copy |
| Signature | Pending | Sign after final review |

### Best habit before submitting
Do one final pass for:
- names and dates
- numbers and IDs
- missing pages
- attachments
- legibility
- consistency across the form

If you want, I can help you make a quick **fill-in checklist** for your specific form.

user_simulator.text:
Mostly fine, but it’s still generic. I don’t need a lecture on “types of forms.” I need a clean way to check the thing without missing something stupid.

What I’d want first is:
- deadline, exact date and time
- whether it has to be received or just postmarked
- every required page, signature, attachment
- anything that can’t be changed once submitted
- a place to note what’s missing and what’s already verified

And I’d keep a separate line for “needs follow-up,” because that’s the part people forget. If you want to be useful, make me a simple checklist or tracking format I can actually use.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a clean, practical format you can use right away.

## Quick check list
- [ ] **Deadline:** exact date
- [ ] **Deadline time:** exact time
- [ ] **Time zone:** if applicable
- [ ] **Submission rule:** must be **received** or only **postmarked**
- [ ] **Required pages:** all pages present
- [ ] **Required signatures:** all signed
- [ ] **Required initials:** all initialed
- [ ] **Attachments:** all included
- [ ] **Supporting docs:** all included
- [ ] **Finality:** anything that can’t be changed after submission noted
- [ ] **Verified items:** confirmed correct
- [ ] **Missing items:** listed clearly
- [ ] **Needs follow-up:** separate line for anything pending

## Simple tracking format
Use this as a working table:

| Item | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Deadline date/time |  |  |
| Received vs postmarked |  |  |
| Page 1 |  |  |
| Page 2 |  |  |
| Signature |  |  |
| Initials |  |  |
| Attachment A |  |  |
| Attachment B |  |  |
| Cannot change after submit |  |  |
| Verified |  |  |
| Missing |  |  |
| Needs follow-up |  |  |

## If you want it even simpler
You can keep three buckets:

### Verified
- deadline confirmed
- submission rule confirmed
- required pages checked
- signatures checked
- attachments checked

### Missing
- [ ] item 1
- [ ] item 2

### Needs follow-up
- [ ] waiting on document
- [ ] waiting on answer
- [ ] waiting on signature

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page fillable template** you can copy/paste.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still a little too tidy, but usable.

What I’d want added is a spot for **who verified it and when**. Otherwise you end up wondering later if you checked it yourself or just thought you did.

Something like:
- Verified by:
- Date/time checked:
- Source of confirmation:

And for the “can’t be changed once submitted” part, I’d want it called out separately, not buried in notes. That’s the kind of thing people miss and then regret.

So yes — close. I’d use it if you gave me the same thing in a plain blank form, not all the explanation around it.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s the plain blank form:

**Must be received by:**

| Item | Verified? | Missing? | Notes |

| Signature |  |  |  |
| Initials |  |  |  |
| Attachment A |  |  |  |
| Attachment B |  |  |  |
| Supporting doc 1 |  |  |  |
| Supporting doc 2 |  |  |  |

---

-

If you want, I can also turn this into a **super-short one-box-per-line version** for notes or a phone screen.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s the thing. Plain, blank, no fluff. I’d use this.

The separate box for **cannot be changed after submission** is the important part. Good that you pulled it out instead of burying it.

This is usable as-is.
